title: Dense per-patch coordinate consensus (corrected re-attempt): from-scratch trunk, no map-cell field, error-driven sampler, disagreement-based confidence
hypothesis: Every one of the last 12 rounds (36-47), including the 5 that swapped the backbone away from mnv3s_features8.pt (41-46), left one thing structurally intact: a SINGLE descriptor per crop feeding ONE decision mechanism that has to pick a location -- a flat 1024-way field (36-40), a coordinate-generated field (39), a coarse-plus-offset field (40), a contrastive/reconstruction-pretrained trunk still feeding the SAME field (41-44), a shortlist-then-rerank comparator (45), or two factorized axis classifiers (46). champion-failure-mode-probes memory shows exactly what that costs: the true cell is the champion field's top-1 pick only ~2% of the time despite landing in its own top-20 (of 1024) 33-58% of crops -- the descriptor already narrows the field down, but no single reparameterization of the final decision (36-40, 45, 46) or of the descriptor feeding it (41-44) has ever closed that gap, because all of them still route through one pooled global vector and one final decision. Exp 47 diagnosed this correctly and proposed the direct fix -- dense per-patch coordinate regression with attention-weighted consensus, in the spirit of CLAUDE.md SS3's named prior art (DSAC/ACE): every one of 64 spatial locations on the trunk's own 8x8 feature grid (x4 for the C4 rotation copies = 256 total) independently proposes its own (u, v) guess plus a reliability weight from ONLY its own local receptive field, no global pooling, no map-cell dictionary at all -- but exp 47 was REJECTED BEFORE IMPLEMENTATION, correctly, because its own self-reported architecture left 'Output (lat, lon, confidence)' and the confusability-weighted sampler untouched, and the pivot rule requires at least one of those two to genuinely change alongside the representational core. That rejection was about compliance, not science -- the dense-consensus idea itself was never trained, never refuted. This is the corrected re-attempt (same pattern as exp 41 -> exp 42's rejected-then-corrected redo): same core scientific bet, but this time Output and the sampler are ACTUALLY rebuilt, not just relabeled, because both mechanisms are structurally tied to the very cell-classification framing this pivot deletes. The champion's confidence came from a small trained sub-network (conf_head MLP + BCE loss against a 'hit within GOOD_ERR_UV' label, calibrated by a shift buffer) reading hand-picked field-shape statistics (peak mass, entropy, sharpened-vs-unsharpened decode gap) that only exist because there IS a field to have a peak and an entropy over. With no field, there is no peak mass and no field entropy to compute -- confidence has to be rebuilt from what the new architecture actually produces: the 256 votes' own agreement. And the confusability-weighted sampler (exp 35, kept 743.07m) is a precomputed, static, per-GRID-CELL descriptor-similarity table -- it has no meaning once there is no grid of classifier rows to bias exposure toward. Both are replaced by mechanisms native to the new architecture rather than removed and left as dead weight: confidence becomes a deterministic (non-learned) statistic of the votes' weighted spread and attention entropy, calibrated by the same post-hoc shift technique as before; the sampler becomes a dynamic, per-individual-training-location EMA of the model's OWN live regression error, oversampling wherever the model is currently doing worst -- a training-DATA-strategy mechanism that only makes sense once locations are scored continuously by direct coordinate error instead of discretely by cell membership.
method: Replace TinyLocNet's ImageNet-pretrained-MobileNetV3-Small-trunk + gated 1024-way classification field + soft-argmax entirely with: (1) a from-scratch 4-stage plain conv trunk (32->64->96->128 channels, stride 2 each, 128x128 -> 8x8x128, no pretrained weights, no torchvision backbone import); (2) a single shared 1x1 conv 'voter head' that reads each of the 64 spatial positions independently and outputs that patch's own (u,v) guess (sigmoid-bounded to [0,1]) plus a raw reliability logit -- run over the crop's own C4 stack (4 exact 90-degree rotations, kept from the champion) for 4x64=256 total independent votes; (3) a learned-temperature softmax over the 256 reliability logits produces attention weights, and the final (u,v) is their weighted mean -- this IS the decode, replacing soft-argmax over a field entirely; (4) confidence is now a deterministic, non-learned function of the 256 votes' attention-weighted spread around their own mean plus the attention distribution's entropy, passed through a fixed linear-then-sigmoid transform with a single calibrated shift buffer (reusing train.py's existing calibrate_conf_shift mechanism unchanged, since it only needs an opaque conf output to threshold against) -- no conf_head module, no BCE loss term, no field-shape statistics; (5) the training signal becomes a Huber loss on the pooled consensus decode, plus a per-vote regression term (each patch's own guess graded against the true location, weighted by its OWN current -- detached -- attention weight so the gradient shapes what patches propose without also being the sole driver of which patches get trusted), plus a small entropy-floor term that penalizes the attention distribution collapsing toward a single dominant vote; (6) the training sampler drops the static confusability-weighted per-cell table entirely and replaces it with a dynamic per-location error EMA: every location carries a running estimate of the model's own current squared coordinate error there, recomputed after every minibatch from that batch's actual predictions, and each epoch's location draw is blended (alpha=0.7 error-weighted / 0.3 uniform) toward wherever the model is currently doing worst -- oversampling hard locations directly by the metric that matters (coordinate error), not by a proxy (static color/texture similarity) that no longer has a corresponding classifier row to protect.
expected_outcome: If the single-descriptor-to-single-decision bottleneck is the real wall the last 12 rounds jointly point at (as exp 47 argued but never tested), worst-case median error improves from the champion's 743.07 m to roughly 500-700 m (6-33%), because a confusable lookalike patch is outvoted by dozens of other patches seeing genuinely distinctive content elsewhere in the same crop, rather than having to be out-ranked by one classifier decision. Mechanism-level signature to check post-hoc: the weighted vote spread should be visibly smaller on correctly-localized eval crops than on badly-localized ones (a real consensus signal), and attention weights should not collapse to one dominant vote on every crop (some crops should show 5-20 votes sharing meaningful weight -- the entropy-floor term is there specifically to keep this option open, not force it). A null or negative result would be informative in a genuinely new way after 12 failed rounds: it would mean the ranking/single-decision framing itself is not the wall, and the next iteration should treat per-patch local feature richness (an information-content problem) as the more likely bottleneck instead of trying yet another way to aggregate or reparameterize a decision.
eli5: Every past attempt asked one 'brain' to look at a whole photo and pick the single most likely spot on the map -- and it kept getting fooled by lookalike neighborhoods, even though it usually had the right answer somewhere in its shortlist. This experiment tries something different: instead of one big vote, the photo is chopped into 64 small patches, and each patch gets to cast its own independent guess about where it is, along with how confident it feels. The patches that seem most reliable get listened to more, and the final answer is basically a weighted democratic vote among all of them (run four times, once per 90-degree rotation, so 256 little voters total). A single tricked-out patch can no longer hijack the whole answer, because dozens of other patches that see something genuinely distinctive can outvote it. Confidence is also rebuilt from scratch: instead of training a separate mini-network to guess whether the answer is trustworthy, we just look directly at whether the 256 voters agree with each other -- lots of agreement means high confidence, a split vote means low confidence.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128x128 px crop, one of 6 lighting renders

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: from-scratch 4-stage plain conv stack (32->64->96->128 channels, stride 2 each stage), 128x128 -> 8x8x128, no pretrained weights, no torchvision backbone

stages.name: Per-patch coordinate voters
stages.detail: a single shared 1x1 conv reads every one of the 64 spatial feature-grid positions independently and outputs that patch's own (u, v) map-coordinate guess plus a reliability weight -- no global pooling, no discrete map-cell dictionary

stages.name: Rotation-robust vote pooling
stages.detail: the same trunk + per-patch voter head runs on the crop's 3 exact 90-degree pixel rotations too; all 4x64=256 individual coordinate votes (not logits over a field) are pooled together into one consensus set

stages.name: Coordinate decode
stages.detail: a learned-temperature softmax over all 256 votes' reliability weights produces attention weights; final (u, v) is the attention-weighted mean of the 256 individual patch guesses -- replaces the old classify-1024-cells-then-soft-argmax decode entirely, no map-cell grid anywhere

stages.name: Output (lat, lon, confidence)
stages.detail: frozen wire contract (1x3x128x128 float32 in, [u, v, conf] out) unchanged, but conf's content is rebuilt: no trained conf sub-network and no BCE-against-hit-label supervision anymore (both required a field's peak-mass/entropy shape that no longer exists) -- conf is now a deterministic transform of the 256 votes' own weighted spread and attention entropy, calibrated only by a single post-hoc shift buffer against the scorer's fixed threshold

stages.name: Training sampler
stages.detail: replaces exp 35's static confusability-weighted per-grid-cell table (precomputed once from reference.tif, no notion of live model error) with a dynamic per-individual-location EMA of the model's own current squared coordinate error, recomputed after every minibatch; each epoch's location draw is blended 0.7 error-weighted / 0.3 uniform toward wherever the model is currently doing worst -- a mechanism that only makes sense once locations are scored continuously by direct error instead of by which discrete classifier cell they fall into

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: Huber loss on the final pooled (u,v) consensus decode + a per-vote regression term weighting each patch's own guess by its own current (detached) attention weight + a small entropy-floor term discouraging the attention distribution from collapsing to one dominant vote -- no field cross-entropy, no confidence BCE term, replacing the champion's Gaussian-smoothed 1024-way CE + coord L2 + conf BCE entirely

implementation_brief:
FIXED CONTRACTS TO PRESERVE EXACTLY: train.py's CLI (--area, --out-dir, --data-dir, --epochs, --max-crops-per-bucket, --seed, unchanged argparse surface and behavior) and model/model.py's ONNX export contract per its module docstring -- export_onnx must still produce, for each area, one ONNX file taking a 1x3x128x128 float32 input in [0,1] and returning a single output tensor shaped [[u, v, conf]] with u,v normalized map coords in [0,1] and conf in [0,1]; opset_version=17, dynamo=False, input_names=['frame'], output_names=['uvc'], exactly as today. The frozen scorer (pipeline/score.py) only ever calls the exported ONNX -- it has zero dependency on model.py internals, so nothing there needs to change.

model/model.py -- full rewrite of the model-specific parts:
- DELETE: `from torchvision.models import mobilenet_v3_small`; module constants GRID_K, TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS, DECODE_BETA, GOOD_ERR_UV, PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH, IMAGENET_MEAN, IMAGENET_STD; functions `_grid_centers` and `_build_pretrained_trunk`; every reference to mnv3s_features8.pt anywhere including prose (the module docstring's Exp 11/12 history paragraphs describing the pretrained trunk and gate must be deleted or rewritten so the literal filename does not appear anywhere in model/ -- autoresearch/backbonecheck.py strips docstrings/comments before matching identifiers+weight-filenames, so a prose mention would NOT trip the gate, but delete it anyway for a clean history; the executable code must contain zero backbone/weights identifiers, which a full rewrite naturally satisfies).
- KEEP UNCHANGED: `_c4_stack(x)` helper (still used, exact same 4-way rotation stacking).
- ADD module constants: `CONF_VAR_SCALE = 200.0`, `CONF_ENT_SCALE = 2.0` (fixed, non-learned scale factors turning vote spread + attention entropy into a confidence logit -- exact calibration is handled downstream by the existing conf_shift mechanism, so these do not need to be precisely tuned), `VOTE_COEF = 0.5` (weight on the per-vote auxiliary regression term), `FLOOR_COEF = 0.02` (weight on the entropy-floor term), `HUBER_DELTA = 0.02` (Huber loss delta in normalized [0,1] map-coordinate units).
- NEW from-scratch trunk (module-level function or inline in `__init__`): `nn.Sequential` of 4 blocks, each `nn.Conv2d(in_ch, out_ch, 3, stride=2, padding=1)` + `nn.BatchNorm2d(out_ch)` + `nn.ReLU(inplace=True)`, channel progression 3->32->64->96->128; verify via a zero-tensor probe (same pattern as today's `probe = self.features(...)`) that 128x128 input yields 8x8 spatial output.
- REWRITE `TinyLocNet.__init__`: build the new trunk as `self.trunk`; `self.voter_head = nn.Conv2d(128, 3, kernel_size=1)` (outputs per-spatial-position [u_raw, v_raw, reliability_logit]); `self.log_temp = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(1))` (learned attention temperature, exp(log_temp) applied at forward time, initialized so temp starts at 1.0); keep `self.register_buffer('conf_shift', torch.zeros(1))`. DELETE: layout_squeeze, loc_logits, dark_logits, gate, conf_head, cell_u/cell_v buffers -- none of these exist in the new design.
- REWRITE `forward(self, x, return_votes: bool = False)`: `n = x.shape[0]`; `x4 = _c4_stack(x)` -> `[4N,3,128,128]`; `fmap = self.trunk(x4)` -> `[4N,128,8,8]`; `raw4 = self.voter_head(fmap).reshape(4, n, 3, 8, 8)`; `uv4 = torch.sigmoid(raw4[:, :, :2])` -> `[4,N,2,8,8]`; `rel4 = raw4[:, :, 2]` -> `[4,N,8,8]`; reshape to per-sample vote sets: `uv = uv4.reshape(4, n, 2, 64).permute(1, 0, 3, 2).reshape(n, 256, 2)`; `rel = rel4.reshape(4, n, 64).permute(1, 0, 2).reshape(n, 256)`; `temp = torch.exp(self.log_temp)`; `w = torch.softmax(rel / temp, dim=1)` -> `[N,256]`; `uv_mean = (w.unsqueeze(-1) * uv).sum(dim=1)` -> `[N,2]`; `var = (w.unsqueeze(-1) * (uv - uv_mean.unsqueeze(1)).pow(2)).sum(dim=(1, 2))` -> `[N]` (weighted total variance, both u and v); `ent = -(w * torch.log(w + 1e-9)).sum(dim=1) / float(np.log(256))` -> `[N]` normalized entropy in roughly [0,1]; `conf = torch.sigmoid(-CONF_VAR_SCALE * var - CONF_ENT_SCALE * ent - self.conf_shift)`; `out = torch.cat([uv_mean, conf.unsqueeze(1)], dim=1)` -> `[N,3]`. If `return_votes` is True, return `(out, (uv, w))`; else return `out` alone (the plain-tensor branch is what `export_onnx` must trace, so ONNX export always uses the default `return_votes=False` call).
- REWRITE `build_model()`: unchanged signature, still `return TinyLocNet()`.
- REWRITE `loss_fn(pred, aux, target_uv)`: new signature -- second argument is now the `(uv_votes, w)` tuple from `return_votes=True`, not field logits. Body: `uv_votes, w = aux`; `consensus_loss = nn.functional.huber_loss(pred[:, :2], target_uv, delta=HUBER_DELTA)`; `per_vote_err = (uv_votes - target_uv.unsqueeze(1)).pow(2).sum(-1)` -> `[N,256]`; `vote_loss = (w.detach() * per_vote_err).sum(dim=1).mean()`; `ent = -(w * torch.log(w + 1e-9)).sum(dim=1) / float(np.log(w.shape[1]))`; `floor_loss = (1.0 - ent).mean()`; `return consensus_loss + VOTE_COEF * vote_loss + FLOOR_COEF * floor_loss`. No confidence term in the loss at all (conf is non-learned, nothing to supervise).
- `export_onnx`: unchanged code (still traces `model(dummy)` with default args, same opset/names/dynamo flag) -- works automatically once forward()'s default branch returns a plain `[1,3]` tensor.
- `estimate_position`: unchanged, still just runs the ONNX session and unpacks `[u, v, conf]`.

model/train.py -- targeted rewrite:
- Change the import line to `from model.model import build_model, export_onnx, loss_fn` (drop `GRID_K`).
- DELETE `compute_cell_weights`, the `NEIGHBOR_EXCLUDE_R` constant, and `CONFUSABILITY_ALPHA` (replace with `ERROR_ALPHA = 0.7`); ADD `EMA_BETA = 0.2`.
- `sample_epoch(...)`: keep the same argument list and per-bucket/per-realization loop structure, but also accumulate a parallel `ids` list -- append the crop's index `i` (the loop variable already iterating `for i in part`) once per drawn crop, in the same order as `xs`/`ys`. Return `x, y, np.array(ids, dtype=np.int64)` (three values instead of two).
- `train_area`: replace the one-time `cell_w = compute_cell_weights(...)` / `crop_cell` / static `crop_probs` block with `loc_err_ema = np.ones(len(crops), dtype=np.float64)` computed once before the epoch loop. Inside the `for epoch in range(total_epochs):` loop, recompute `crop_probs` fresh each epoch from the current `loc_err_ema` (uniform = `np.full(len(crops), 1.0/len(crops))`; `weighted = loc_err_ema / loc_err_ema.sum()`; `crop_probs = ERROR_ALPHA * weighted + (1 - ERROR_ALPHA) * uniform`; renormalize to sum 1) BEFORE calling `sample_epoch`, and capture the returned `ids` array alongside `x, y`. In the minibatch loop, after `out, aux = model(xb, return_votes=True)` and `loss = loss_fn(out, aux, yb)` and the optimizer step, add: `with torch.no_grad(): per_err = ((out[:, :2] - yb) ** 2).sum(dim=1).cpu().numpy()`; `batch_ids = ids[idx.numpy() if torch.is_tensor(idx) else idx]`; `loc_err_ema[batch_ids] = (1 - EMA_BETA) * loc_err_ema[batch_ids] + EMA_BETA * per_err`. Replace the two-group optimizer (`trunk_params` at 1e-4 / `head_params` at 1e-3`) with a single group: `opt = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3)` (no pretrained trunk left to protect with a slower LR) -- the `sched = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(...)` line stays as-is, just wraps the single-group optimizer now. Update the `info` dict: drop `confusability_alpha` / `cell_weight_ratio_p90_p10`, add `"error_alpha": ERROR_ALPHA` and `"loc_err_ema_ratio_p90_p10": float(np.quantile(loc_err_ema, 0.9) / np.quantile(loc_err_ema, 0.1))`; change `"init"` to `"from-scratch (no pretrained backbone)"`.
- `calibrate_conf_shift`: leave the function body entirely unchanged -- it already treats `out[:, 2]` as an opaque conf value to threshold in logit space and set `model.conf_shift`, and that contract holds regardless of how conf was computed inside forward(). It calls `model(xb)` with default args, which is exactly the `return_votes=False` branch.
- Update the module docstring's running history to add one short paragraph for this experiment (mirroring the existing Exp 7/11/12/... style), noting the pivot away from the map-cell field to per-patch consensus and the sampler/confidence rebuild -- but do NOT leave the literal string `mnv3s_features8.pt` anywhere in the file even in prose.

Do not touch pipeline/ (frozen), autoresearch/ (harness), or pipeline/score.py's gates -- this design's parameter count (a ~185k-param from-scratch trunk plus a ~390-param 1x1 conv head) is far under the 4 MiB ONNX budget, and the four-forward-pass-per-input (C4 stack) latency profile is the same shape the champion already uses today, so no gate gymnastics are needed.
